title: 10 Effective Herbal Creams to Treat a Blocked Nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole helps to open up a blocked nose by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause congestion, making it easier to breathe.
Eucalyptol is good for blocked nose because it helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Quercetin is good for a blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing.
Tannins are good for a blocked nose because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Polyphenols are good for blocked nose because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a popular remedy for a blocked nose. This is because it contains eucalyptus oil, which is known for its decongestant properties. When you apply the cream to your nose, the eucalyptus oil is quickly absorbed into the skin and released into the air. It then works to thin and clear out excess mucus and debris that's clogging your nasal passages. This helps to open up your airways and make it easier to breathe. The eucalyptus oil also has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages. As a result, you may find that your blocked nose clears up quickly after using the cream.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal cream that helps with a blocked nose, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, dry the Ocimum basilicum leaves in a low-temperature oven (around 35-40 degrees Celsius) for several hours or until they are completely dry. Then, grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2-3 tablespoons of the basil powder with 2 tablespoons of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a small bowl. Heat the mixture gently over low heat (around 30-40 degrees Celsius) for a few minutes to help the ingredients combine. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Once it has cooled, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any lumps or particles. Finally, mix the strained mixture with a moisturizing cream or lotion, such as shea butter or aloe vera gel, to create a soothing and fragrant cream that can be applied to the nose to help relieve congestion and ease breathing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease congestion.
Carnosic acid is good for blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Quercetin is good for blocked nose because it helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary oil cream, is a natural remedy that can help with a blocked nose. The main reason for its effectiveness is that it contains eucalyptus oil, which is a natural decongestant. This means it helps to break down the thick mucus that blocks the airways, allowing for easier breathing.  The menthol in the cream also helps to thin out the mucus, making it easier to expel. When applied to the nose, the cream's cooling sensation can help to reduce swelling and open up the nasal passages. As the cream works to loosen and clear the blockage, you may start to feel a sense of relief from congestion.  Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in rosemary oil can help to reduce swelling and redness in the nasal passages, further easing congestion. When used regularly, the cream can also help to prevent future blockages by keeping the nasal passages clear and open.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal cream to help with a blocked nose, you will need some fresh or dried Ocimum basilicum leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a natural cream base. First, dry the Ocimum basilicum leaves if they are fresh, or use the dried ones directly. Then, mix a tablespoon of dried leaves with a cup of carrier oil in a small saucepan and heat it gently on low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the oil to absorb the herbs' properties. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Now, let the oil cool down and mix it with an equal amount of natural cream base, like coconut cream or shea butter cream. Stir well to combine and transfer the mixture into a clean container. Store it in the fridge to keep it fresh for up to a week. When you need to use it, simply apply a small amount to your nostrils to help ease congestion and open up your airways.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to open up airways and reduce swelling in the nasal passages.
Rosmarinic acid is good for blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease congestion.
Carvacrol is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to break down mucus and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a popular herb used in many medicines. Its creams are especially helpful for people with a blocked nose. The active ingredient in thyme, called thymol, has strong antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to the nose, it helps to reduce swelling and fight off any infections that might be causing the blockage. Thymol also has a decongestant effect, which means it helps to thin and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. This makes Thymus vulgaris cream a great option for people who experience congestion due to colds, allergies, or sinus infections. The cream is also gentle on the skin and can be used regularly without any side effects. By reducing swelling and fighting off infections, Thymus vulgaris cream helps to clear the airways and promote easier breathing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal creams for a blocked nose, start by gathering dried Ocimum basilicum leaves. You can buy these from a health food store or online. Next, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Ocimum basilicum leaves with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After the mixture has cooled down, strain it into a clean bowl to remove the leaves. Now, mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or shea butter with the cooled Ocimum basilicum tea in the bowl. Stir well until the mixture is smooth and even. Finally, transfer the mixture into a small jar and store it in the fridge. To use the cream, apply a small amount to your nose and gently massage it in. The eucalyptol in Ocimum basilicum will help to open up your airways and clear your blocked nose.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for blocked nose because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, allowing for easy breathing and relieving congestion.

Rosmarinic acid is good for blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Menthone is good for blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to open up airways and reduce swelling in the nasal passages.
Luteolin is good for blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams are good for a blocked nose because they contain a natural ingredient called menthol. Menthol is a type of oil that comes from peppermint, a plant that helps to open up airways in the nose. When you apply Mentha x piperita creams to your nose, the menthol in it starts to work quickly. It helps to thin down mucus, which is the thick liquid that can block your nose. By thinning down mucus, menthol makes it easier for you to breathe through your nose. It also helps to reduce swelling in the nose, which can be a cause of a blocked nose. This makes it easier for air to pass through, helping you to breathe more easily.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal cream to help with a blocked nose, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Ocimum basilicum leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a few drops of essential oil like eucalyptus. First, heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a saucepan over low heat until the beeswax is fully melted. Add the dried Ocimum basilicum leaves to the mixture and let it steep for about 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the leaves from the oil. Then, add a few drops of eucalyptus essential oil to help open up the nasal passages. Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool completely before applying it to the skin, paying special attention to the areas around the nostrils. This Ocimum basilicum herbal cream can be applied as needed to help ease congestion and promote a clear, easy breathing.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for a blocked nose because it helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and opens up airways, allowing for easier breathing.
Linalyl acetate is good for blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help open up airways and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Caryophyllene is good for blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Geraniol is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, is a popular herb used to make creams and oils. It has natural decongestant properties that can help relieve a blocked nose. The active compounds in lavender oil, like linalool and linalyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This can help open up the airways and make it easier to breathe. When applied topically as a cream, lavender oil can also help to relax the muscles in the face and reduce congestion caused by tension. Additionally, lavender has antibacterial properties that can help fight off infections that may be contributing to the blocked nose. As a result, using Lavandula angustifolia cream may provide relief from a blocked nose and make it easier to breathe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Ocimum basilicum (basil) herbal cream that helps with a blocked nose, you will first need to make a herbal infusion by mixing 1 tablespoon of dried basil leaves with 1 cup of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the basil's active compounds to dissolve into the water, then strain it into a bowl using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Once the infusion has cooled, add 2 tablespoons of a base cream like coconut oil or shea butter to the bowl, followed by 2 teaspoons of beeswax, and mix well until the beeswax has melted. Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil and 2 drops of eucalyptus essential oil to the mixture and continue stirring until it has thickened to a smooth, creamy consistency. Finally, transfer the herbal cream into a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to help ease congestion and open up your airways when applied topically to your nose.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Quercetin is good for blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Thymol is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including a blocked nose. The cream made from this plant contains a compound called eucalyptol, which helps to loosen and clear out mucus and other debris that can clog up the nasal passages. When applied topically, the cream can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nose, making it easier to breathe. The eucalyptol in Salvia officinalis cream also has a decongestant effect, which means it can help to reduce the amount of fluid in the nose, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing discomfort and making it easier to breathe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal creams that help with a blocked nose, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, gather a handful of fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves, also known as sweet basil, and dry them in a warm, dark place until they are completely dry. Next, combine the dried leaves with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan, and heat it gently over low heat for about 10 minutes. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly, then strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Add a few drops of beeswax to the oil and stir well to create a thick and creamy texture. Finally, add a few drops of your preferred essential oil, such as eucalyptus or peppermint, to help relieve congestion. Pour the mixture into small containers and store them in the fridge for up to a month. When you need to use it, simply apply a small amount to your nostrils and inhale deeply to help clear your sinuses and breathe easily.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Roscmarinic acid is good for blocked nose because it helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and has natural decongestant properties that relieve congestion.
Quercetin is good for a blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses.
Tannins are good for blocked nose because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyssopus officinalis, also known as hyssop, is a plant with healing properties that can help with a blocked nose. The essential oils in hyssop have natural decongestant properties, which can help to break down mucus and reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This makes it easier to breathe and can provide relief from congestion. The anti-inflammatory properties of hyssop can also help to soothe the nasal tissues and reduce irritation. When applied as a cream, the hyssop oil can be absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from a blocked nose. Additionally, hyssop has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause infections in the nasal passages. This makes hyssop cream a popular natural remedy for people looking to alleviate congestion and sinus problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal cream for blocked nose, start by drying the Ocimum basilicum leaves, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator on a low heat setting. Once dried, grind the leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the basil powder with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:2 ratio, so if you have one tablespoon of basil powder, use two tablespoons of carrier oil. Heat the oil mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan with a bowl on top, stirring occasionally, until the oil is warm and fragrant. Remove the oil mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before straining it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to the oil mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a jar or container and let it cool and set completely before applying it to your nose to help ease congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Hyssopus officinalis herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for a blocked nose because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Linalool is good for blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Terpinen-4-ol is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to break down and clear mucus and other substances that can cause congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil cream, is good for a blocked nose. The active ingredient in this cream, melaleuca oil, has strong antibacterial properties. When you apply the cream to your nose, it helps to kill the bacteria that cause congestion. This allows the mucus in your nose to break down and drain out more easily, making it easier to breathe. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. This can make it feel like you can breathe more easily, even when your nose is blocked. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, so it's suitable for most people, including those with sensitive skin. When you use the cream regularly, you may find that your blocked nose becomes less frequent and less severe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal cream that helps with a blocked nose, you will need fresh basil leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, dry the basil leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture. Once dry, chop the leaves into small pieces and mix them with 1 cup of coconut oil in a blender or food processor. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the oil is warm and the basil is fully infused. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a separate double boiler, then slowly add it to the basil-infused oil, stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and cools, then transfer it to a jar or tin and let it solidify. Once set, your Ocimum basilicum herbal cream is ready to use: simply apply a small amount to your nose and inhale the fragrance to help ease congestion and open up your airways.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for a blocked nose because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.
Iridoids are good for blocked nose because they help to relax the muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Polyphenols in Ocimum Basilicum are good for a blocked nose because they help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream can help with a blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you have a blocked nose, it's usually because of swelling in the nasal passages. Echinacea purpurea reduces swelling by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation. This makes it easier to breathe and can relieve congestion.  The cream also has antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off infections that can cause a blocked nose. When you have a cold or flu, your body's immune system is weakened, and you're more likely to catch an infection. Echinacea purpurea cream can help to boost your immune system by reducing the severity of colds and flu.  Echinacea purpurea cream can also be used to relieve sinus pressure and headaches caused by a blocked nose. The cream can be applied directly to the nose and sinuses to provide quick relief. Some people also find it helpful to apply the cream to the chest and back to relieve congestion from the inside. Using Echinacea purpurea cream can be a good alternative to nasal sprays and decongestants, which can have side effects like drowsiness and headaches. The cream is also easy to apply and can be used as often as needed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal creams that help with a blocked nose, you'll need some fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, a double boiler, and a few other simple ingredients. First, dry a bunch of fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves in a warm, dark place or use a dehydrator to preserve them. Once dry, finely chop the leaves and mix them with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a bowl, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Let this mixture steep for a few hours or overnight in the fridge, allowing the Ocimum basilicum's properties to infuse into the oil. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler and add the infused oil to it, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your Ocimum basilicum herbal cream is ready to use - simply apply a small amount to your nose or inhale the aroma to help ease congestion and breathe more easily.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for blocked nose

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for blocked nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and make breathing easier.
Linalool is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to open airways and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Eugenol is good for a blocked nose because it has natural decongestant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Oleanolic acid is good for a blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and open up airways, allowing for easier breathing.
Apigenin is good for a blocked nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum, also known as sweet basil, has been used for centuries to help relieve a blocked nose. The plant's essential oils, particularly eugenol and linalool, have natural decongestant properties. When applied as a cream or oil to the nostrils, these oils can help to loosen and clear mucus, reducing congestion. This can provide quick relief from stuffiness and sinus pressure. Additionally, eugenol has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and promote healing. By using Ocimum basilicum cream, the nostrils can breathe more easily, and the sinuses can drain more efficiently, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ocimum basilicum herbal creams that help with a blocked nose, you will need some fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, dry a handful of fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator until they are completely dry and crumbly. Then, grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, melt the coconut oil in a double boiler, add the ground Ocimum basilicum powder to it, and stir well to combine. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add the melted beeswax to it and stir until the mixture thickens to a creamy consistency. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean jar and let it cool and set before using it as a nasal decongestant cream. To use it, simply apply a small amount to the nose and inhale deeply to help clear your nasal passages.
remedies.image_alt: Ocimum basilicum herbal creams for blocked nose

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great solution for people suffering from a blocked nose. One of the key ingredients in these creams is Eucalyptus globulus, also known as eucalyptus oil. This herb has natural decongestant properties that help to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary, is another herb often used in herbal creams for blocked noses. It has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion and sinus pressure.  A blocked nose can be a real nuisance, making it hard to sleep, work, and enjoy everyday activities. When your nose is congested, you may feel tired, irritable, and uncomfortable. This can affect your relationships, work performance, and overall well-being. By using herbal creams containing Eucalyptus globulus and Rosmarinus officinalis, you can help to clear out your nasal passages and breathe more easily. This can improve your quality of life, allowing you to feel more energized, focused, and confident. By addressing the issue of a blocked nose, you can reduce your reliance on medications and breathe more easily, making it easier to tackle your daily tasks and enjoy your life to the fullest.
supplementary_best: For a blocked nose, a combination of Eucalyptus globulus and Thymus vulgaris in a cream is effective. Eucalyptus globulus has natural decongestant properties due to its eucalyptol content, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. Thymus vulgaris, on the other hand, has antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that may be causing the blockage.  When used together, these two herbs work in harmony to provide relief from congestion. Eucalyptus globulus' decongestant properties help to clear the nasal passages, allowing the antimicrobial properties of Thymus vulgaris to reduce inflammation and fight off infections.  However, adding Mentha x piperita to this combination can enhance its effectiveness. Mentha x piperita has menthol content, which also acts as a natural decongestant, helping to further clear the nasal passages and reduce congestion.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a blocked nose, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One such example is creams containing Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita is not mentioned, but peppermint oil is often made from it). Peppermint oil can be too strong and may make congestion worse. It can also irritate the skin and cause discomfort.  Another example is creams containing Garlic oil (Allium sativum). Garlic oil can be overpowering and may trigger an allergic reaction. Its strong smell can also be overwhelming and may not provide the relief you need.  You should also be cautious with creams containing Ginger oil (Zingiber officinale). While ginger is generally considered safe, it can cause skin irritation in some people. Its strong, spicy smell can also be overwhelming.  Lastly, creams containing Clove oil (Syzygium aromaticum) should be used with caution. Clove oil can be too potent and may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. Its strong, pungent smell can also be overwhelming.  When using herbal creams, it's essential to read the labels carefully and follow the instructions. If you experience any discomfort or worsening of symptoms, stop using the cream and consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_other_remedies: A blocked nose, or congestion, can be relieved with various natural remedies. Steam inhalation is one way to help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. To do this, you breathe in the warm, moist air from a bowl of hot water or a steam shower, which helps to reduce swelling and open up airways. This is because the steam increases the temperature in the nasal passages, making the mucus thinner and easier to expel. You can also add eucalyptus oil or menthol to the water for added benefits.  Another remedy is to drink plenty of fluids, especially warm liquids like tea or broth, which help to thin out mucus and promote its removal. Drinking warm water with a slice of lemon or honey can also provide relief, as the acidity of the lemon and the soothing properties of honey can help to break down mucus and reduce congestion. In addition, a warm compress or a hot pack applied to the face can also help to loosen mucus and improve breathing.  Essential oils such as eucalyptus, peppermint, and lavender can be inhaled directly from a cloth or tissue, or added to a warm bath to help clear congestion. Some people also use humidifiers at home to add moisture to the air, which helps to relieve congestion and coughs. This is because dry air can exacerbate congestion, while moist air helps to thin out mucus and promote its removal.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with a blocked nose often experience other health issues due to the accumulation of mucus and congestion in their sinuses. One common problem is headaches, which can be caused by the pressure and swelling in the sinuses. Medicinal herbs like feverfew and butterbur can help reduce inflammation and ease pain.  Another issue is sinusitis, an infection of the sinuses that can be caused by bacteria or viruses. Echinacea and goldenseal have antimicrobial properties that can help fight off these infections. Some people with a blocked nose also experience fatigue, which can be due to the body's reduced ability to breathe properly and take in oxygen. Ginseng and ashwagandha are adaptogenic herbs that can help increase energy levels.  People with a blocked nose may also experience coughing and wheezing due to the accumulation of mucus in their lungs. Thyme and eucalyptus have decongestant and expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear out mucus. Additionally, some people with a blocked nose may experience sinus-related eye problems, such as redness and itching, due to the spread of infection to the eyes. Eyebright and calendula can help soothe and protect the eyes.  A blocked nose can also be a symptom of allergies, which can be caused by an overactive immune system. Quercetin and stinging nettle are herbs that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.
